active_ingredients: Naphazoline HCl 0.025% (a decongestant/vasoconstrictor) and pheniramine maleate 0.3% (an antihistamine) ophthalmic solution - allergy eye drops (e.g., Visine-A, Naphcon-A, Opcon-A)
indications_and_usage: Uses: temporarily relieves itching and redness of the eye due to pollen, ragweed, grass, animal hair, and dander (allergic conjunctivitis); the antihistamine eases the itch and the decongestant whitens the eye by constricting blood vessels.
contraindications: Do not use if you have narrow-angle glaucoma (the decongestant can precipitate an acute attack) without a doctor's advice; do not use if allergic to any ingredient; do not use to treat contact-lens-related discomfort.
warnings_and_cautions: For ophthalmic use only. Overuse can cause rebound redness (the eye becomes more red and dependent on the drops) - do not use for more than 72 hours in a row. Pupils may become enlarged temporarily. Remove contact lenses before use. Ask a doctor before use if you have heart disease, high blood pressure, narrow-angle glaucoma, or trouble urinating due to an enlarged prostate (the decongestant can be absorbed). Stop use and ask a doctor if you have eye pain, vision changes, continued redness or irritation, or if the condition worsens or lasts more than 72 hours. Do not touch the dropper tip to any surface.
adverse_reactions: Transient stinging or burning, dilated pupils, blurred vision, rebound redness with overuse; rarely systemic effects (raised blood pressure, palpitations, headache), and in young children who ingest it, serious CNS depression.
drug_interactions: MAO inhibitors and tricyclic antidepressants (potential pressor effect from absorbed naphazoline); other systemic anticholinergics/antihistamines (additive effects).
dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 6 years and over: put 1-2 drops in the affected eye(s) up to 4 times daily; do not use for more than 72 hours continuously. Children under 6 years: ask a doctor (and keep out of reach - swallowing these drops can be dangerous to small children).
overdosage: Swallowing the drops, especially by a child, can cause naphazoline (imidazoline) toxicity: drowsiness, sweating, very slow heartbeat, low body temperature, low blood pressure, and even coma. Seek emergency care and contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222) immediately.
